title: GSAP - Awwwards SOTD
notes: Award reference for expressive but controlled animation language.

analysis.layout: Large central identity moment with supporting modules arranged as evidence below the fold.
analysis.typography: Confident display type, short labels, and compressed supporting copy keep the first view direct.
analysis.whitespace: Bold negative space around the brand mark gives motion room to breathe.
analysis.color: Very tight two-color discipline creates instant recognizability.
analysis.motion: Motion feels elastic and highly timed, with object behavior doing the storytelling.
analysis.interaction: Hover and scroll affordances reward exploration without overexplaining.
analysis.usable: Use the idea of one memorable kinetic science object paired with minimal instructional text.
analysis.mustNotCopy: Do not copy the GSAP brand colors, logo, characters, screenshots, or exact elastic choreography.

principles: One visual thesis should dominate the viewport.
Motion should clarify material behavior, not decorate every element.
A tiny palette can feel premium when contrast is decisive.
Short interface labels outperform explanatory clutter.
Interactive objects need stable surrounding typography.
takeaways: Make Gibbs energy feel like a live balance, not a stock illustration.
Use one accent color for feasibility and one neutral field for calculation.
Keep controls quiet so the scientific scene carries the sense of motion.

title: Made With GSAP - Awwwards SOTD
notes: Reference for compact animated examples and dark editorial rhythm.

analysis.layout: Dense index-like modules are balanced by a strong title system and restrained background.
analysis.typography: Utility labels are small and precise while titles stay large and graphic.
analysis.whitespace: Whitespace appears as gutters and pauses between motion samples rather than empty decoration.
analysis.color: Dark surface with a single bright accent gives strong hierarchy.
analysis.motion: Scroll and reveal motion has a catalog feel: repeatable, crisp, and inspectable.
analysis.interaction: The page invites sampling; every module feels like a deliberate specimen.
analysis.usable: The worksheet can treat examples as specimens with consistent rhythm and reveal timing.
analysis.mustNotCopy: Do not copy the dark-neon palette, names, animation examples, or any site thumbnails.

principles: A catalog can still feel editorial if hierarchy is strict.
Bright accents work best when used sparingly.
Repeated modules need one shared geometry.
Motion samples should be inspectable and not chaotic.
Small metadata can add seriousness when typeset consistently.
takeaways: Use calculation rows as disciplined specimens rather than loose cards.
Limit bright color to active progress and the result sign.
Let answer checking reveal with crisp, repeatable motion.

title: WEBGL - Awwwards Collection

analysis.layout: Reference tiles emphasize scene previews and concise categorization.

principles: Visual previews should be large enough to read behavior.
Interface chrome should recede behind the scientific scene.
Category labels can be useful when they are not decorative pills.
A grid needs strong alignment to avoid visual noise.
Motion references need still-state clarity.
takeaways: The scientific visual should be sized as a primary teaching surface.
Section navigation should act like a quiet concept index.
Avoid tiny decorative molecule previews that cannot be inspected.
